factors.name: Alphabet talent exodus & AI concerns
factors.summary: Alphabet shed $269B market cap as two high-profile AI leaders including a Nobel laureate departed for rivals. Markets pricing NDX component GOOGL weakness, cited as pacing S&P/Nasdaq down.

factors.name: Netflix structural weakness
factors.summary: Netflix down 5.6% today to lowest since Oct 2024, nearly cut in half since July on disruption concerns and AI-generated content quality risks. Tech multiple compression signal.

factors.name: SpaceX post-IPO unwind
factors.summary: SpaceX shares down 9-10.5% in third consecutive day of losses after record IPO; now underwater for most retail buyers. Company filing $20B bond sale signals capital needs; high-profile weakness in Musk portfolio.

factors.name: Fed hawkish hold through 2026-2027
factors.summary: Morgan Stanley and TD Securities expect Fed to hold rates through 2026-2027, contradicting market expectations of ~1.6 cuts by year-end. Higher real rates compress long-duration growth valuations.

factors.name: Alan Greenspan death / Fed leadership transition
factors.summary: Greenspan died at 100; legacy debate continues on 2008 financial crisis contribution. New Fed regime under Warsh noted as inflation-fighting credible but markets uncertain on execution.

factors.name: Mixed earnings/guidance outlook
factors.summary: Columbia Threadneedle sees tech rally extending on AI capex acceleration, but no fresh mega-cap earnings surprises in today's window. Near-term sentiment split.

factors.name: Semiconductor strength amid mega-cap weakness
factors.summary: Super Micro rallying on Nvidia partnership; semiconductor ETF (SMH, SOXX) showing relative strength vs communication services. But breadth concern as GOOGL/META/TSLA stumble.

factors.name: Oil/geopolitical relief (Iran waiver)
factors.summary: US waives 60-day Iran sanctions, 30M barrels flooding market, oil down to ~$73.67 near 200-day MA. Risk-off pressure eases slightly; supports energy stability but cuts energy sector cyclical upside.

factors.name: Dollar strength (hawkish Fed expectations)
factors.summary: EUR/USD near 3-month lows at 1.1426; USD/JPY briefly touched 161.90 (40-year high). Strong dollar pressures international revenue for NDX components (MSFT, AAPL, NVDA have ~50-60% overseas sales).

factors.name: PCE inflation data imminent
factors.summary: Markets cautious ahead of US PCE inflation print (mentioned as upcoming catalyst). Surprise on either side could reprice Fed expectations and trigger volatility in growth/rates correlation.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq 100 is in early stages of a pullback (-0.56% 24h, -0.01% 4h) driven by cascading mega-cap weakness: Alphabet's $269B wipeout on AI talent flight, Netflix structural concerns, and SpaceX post-IPO unwinding. The underlying macro story is shifting from AI capex optimism to Fed hawkish hold expectations (rates through 2026-2027), which compresses long-duration growth multiples. Meanwhile, semiconductor names show relative resilience, but breadth is negative. Dollar strength on hawkish positioning adds headwind to international-heavy NDX components.
catalysts_ahead: US PCE inflation data (expected within 24-72h window)
FOMC minutes or speaker commentary on rate trajectory
Mega-cap earnings guidance: MSFT, AAPL, GOOGL, AMZN in coming weeks
UK political/fiscal uncertainty after Starmer exit (potential GBP/USD spillover to risk sentiment)
screening.risk_warning: INVALIDATION: If PCE comes in cool (below expectations), Fed messaging turns dovish, or mega-cap earnings beat with strong forward guidance, the bias flips BULLISH and NDX re-tests 30400-30600. Semiconductor strength could spill into broader tech if NVDA/AMD earnings surprise high. Also monitor VXN (Nasdaq vol) — if it spikes >20, indicates panic; if it stays <18, pullback may be orderly and shallow.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is already underway (down 0.56% 24h); wait for retest of 30300-30350 or close below 30150 before shorting. Better entry on any bounce back to 30400 resistance with deteriorating breadth indicators. Avoid chasing short into PCE print (too much event risk).

factors.name: Mega-cap Tech Weakness
factors.summary: GOOG -5.02%, AMZN -4.75%, MSFT -3.18%, NFLX -5.82% leading selloff today. Alphabet pacing worst day in a year on AI researcher exits. Multiple mega-caps down 3-6% exerts direct downward pressure on NDX.

factors.name: NASDAQ Index Performance
factors.summary: NASDAQ fell sharply with -1.32% decline per investingLive report. NDX -0.33% in 24h despite +0.20% in 4h suggests intraday bounce off support after broader selloff.

factors.name: Nvidia Earnings Headwinds
factors.summary: NVDA facing structural headwinds: analyst notes 'ability to beat expectations has gotten much smaller' as size increases; Kalshi traders betting chip prices decline; Nvidia ranking 'dead last' in chip index despite doubling this year per MarketWatch.

factors.name: SpaceX IPO Unwinding
factors.summary: SpaceX stock down -16.43% today, reversing most of post-IPO rally to $225.64; shares now below IPO closing price of $150 with bond sale announcement adding pressure. Major ETF flow disruptions from IPO arb trades rattle funds.

factors.name: Fed Hawkish Re-pricing
factors.summary: More hawkish-than-expected FOMC decision per investingLive; Fed repricing lifting USD/JPY volatility and Treasury yields; Kevin Warsh (new Fed Chair) seen as supportive of bond market leading rate guidance. Real yields rising compresses growth-stock multiples.

factors.name: 10-Year Yield / Real Yields
factors.summary: Rising US Treasury yields driven by hawkish Fed repricing and higher inflation expectations (Canada CPI +3.2% vs +3.0% expected). Long-duration tech (NQ heavyweights) highly sensitive to real yield expansion.

factors.name: Dollar Strength
factors.summary: USD Index near 101.00, +0.26% on day. Stronger dollar headwind for NDX components with heavy international revenue (MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN, META). Iran talks and oil decline providing only modest relief.

factors.name: Negative Sentiment in AI / Big Tech
factors.summary: Alphabet losing high-profile AI researchers; Netflix pricing in disruption with stock down 5.6% to lowest since October 2024; analyst commentary on AI consciousness becoming 'political battleground.' Meta WhatsApp head stepping down. Confidence in growth narrative slipping.

factors.name: Semiconductor Demand Concerns
factors.summary: Micron (memory) stock focal point for AI capex trajectory uncertainty; Kalshi traders wagering chip pricing declines; chip index doubled but heavy-hitters like NVDA struggling. Forward guidance on hyperscaler capex critical and missing confidence.

factors.name: Prior Bias Continuation
factors.summary: 2026-06-22 17:40 read was 'SELL 6/10 (no confirmation triggered)'. Today's -1.32% NASDAQ decline and mega-cap rout provides partial confirmation, though initial sell signal lacked conviction.

screening.narrative: NAS100 is in early-to-mid stages of a broad mega-cap tech selloff driven by: (1) Nvidia structural headwinds and analyst growth-expectation resets; (2) multiple FAANG constituents down 3–6% on AI researcher exits, disruption narratives, and valuation compression from rising real yields; (3) Fed hawkish repricing pushing Treasury yields higher and squeezing long-duration growth multiples; (4) secondary SPX weakness in high-beta plays like SpaceX (IPO unwind). The 4h bounce (+0.20%) masks a deteriorating 24h trend (-0.33%). Macro environment increasingly hostile: stronger USD, higher real yields, and flagging hyperscaler capex confidence form a toxic backdrop for NQ. Prior SELL signal from June 22 17:40 is now being confirmed in real-time.
catalysts_ahead: Asia PMI flash data (June 23 early morning US time) — could shift inflation narrative
Micron earnings (this week) — AI capex demand litmus test; could re-rate entire NDX if disappointing
Cerebras earnings (this week) — AI infrastructure demand signal
Super Micro heavy options activity — expiration risk and gamma volatility ahead
Any FOMC member commentary on inflation trajectory or rate cut delays — could extend real-yield rise
10-year Treasury auction dynamics — if bid strength weak, yields could spike further
China economic data / Taiwan tensions updates — geopolitical risk to semiconductor supply chain
screening.risk_warning: Intraday bounce from support (30,100–30,200) could extend if rotation into mega-cap beaten-down names (MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN) reverses sentiment. If Iran peace talks collapse or oil prices spike, inflation concerns could re-anchor higher and trigger 'risk-off' bid into bonds rather than equities—which would actually stabilize real yields and reduce pressure on NQ multiples. Surprise hawkish FOMC pivot already priced in, but dovish hold at next meeting could spark explosive short squeeze. Major earnings beats from NVDA, MSFT, or AAPL in coming weeks could invalidate this SELL thesis entirely.
screening.entry_guidance: Intraday short exposure on retest of 30,500–30,700 resistance; prefer to wait for close below 30,100 support before adding. Move already -1.32% intraday—avoid FOMO chasing into extended move. If NDX closes decisively below 30,000 on volume, fresh SHORT at 30,200 with stop at 30,600 is warranted. Pullback entries more attractive than breakdowns at this phase.
